summary: No-ship: the CAS writer and park/unpark flows still have material lost-update, sibling-loss, and active+parked drift paths.

findings.title: Malformed existing ~/.claude.json is collapsed to {} and overwritten
findings.recommendation: Abort applyMcpDelta on an existing but unparsable/non-object file instead of treating it as empty. Only synthesize {} for ENOENT; otherwise leave the live bytes untouched and surface a write error.

findings.title: CAS has a clobber window between the compare read and atomic write
findings.recommendation: Do not treat read-back-equals-our-bytes as proof no update was lost. Use a commit protocol that cannot replace the target if it changed after the comparison, or serialize all writers through a real lock/transaction before claiming CAS safety.

findings.title: MCP rename is split into two deltas and can abort after deleting the old server
findings.recommendation: Make rename a single transactional delta that removes the old key and sets the new key in one guarded write, with target-exists checks revalidated on every retry.

findings.title: MCP disable parks a stale snapshot outside the guarded retry
findings.recommendation: Capture the config being parked inside the same retry transaction that removes it from ~/.claude.json. If the active server changes during retry, abort or re-park the new exact config instead of deleting live state while retaining an old parked copy.

findings.title: Hook disable/enable are two-file blind writes with no rollback or recovery marker
findings.recommendation: Serialize or CAS the parked hook store and add a recoverable pending state. If settings removal fails after parking, roll back the parked entry; on crash recovery, complete or roll back so identical active+parked groups cannot persist.

findings.title: Reconcile adopts active records even when the same primitive is also parked
findings.recommendation: Check composition invariants before HV7 adoption, or carry an explicit active-and-parked drift signal from primitive-state so reconcile defers/reports those records instead of listing them as cleanly adopted enabled primitives.
